revision.text: {{Div col|colwidth=45em}} ; General trivia : {{tlx|Trivia}} – Suggests relocation of the relevant details in a trivia section, to another section (or article). : {{tlx|Trivia section}} – Same as above, but goes at the top of the trivia section, rather than the top of the article. ; Relevance and importance : {{tlx|Off topic}} – For a section that has wandered from the topic of the article : {{tlx|Content}} – For an article or section containing information whose relevance is disputed. : {{tlx|Importance section}} – For a section with information that is simply off-topic for the article, and needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Importance inline}} – For a particular item that is off-topic needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Self-sourcing examples}} – For an article or section with poorly cited examples. : {{tlx|Better source example}} – For a particular instance of a poorly cited example. : {{tlx|Relevance inline}} – For a particular item that doesn't seem to belong in the context at all. : {{tlx|Non sequitur}} – For a namedropping of someone or something the relevance of which may not be clear to the reader. ; Excessive detail : {{tlx|Overly detailed}} – For excessive focus on minute details not of interest to our general readership. : {{tlx|Summarize section}} – For sections that are too detailed and need to be summarized. : {{tlx|Example farm}} – For excessive use of examples. : {{tlx|Too many see alsos}} – For an indiscriminate "See also" section, most of which should be pruned or integrated into the prose. ; Topical trivia : {{tlx|In popular culture}} – For excessive "popular culture" and "media references" sorts of material. : {{tlx|Fiction trivia}} (or {{tlx|In-universe}}) – For too many trivial fictional references (or too much "in-universe" detail). : {{tlx|Long plot}}, {{tlx|All plot}} – For excessively detailed plot summaries. : {{tlx|Cleanup book}}, {{tlx|Cleanup film}} – For excessive detail about particular types of works (other than plot and fictional or in-universe issues). : {{tlx|Game trivia}} – For too much gaming-related trivia. : {{tlx|MOSLOW}} – For list that does not follow the Manual of Style for lists of works, e.g. not in chronological order : {{tlx|Cleanup university}}, {{tlx|Cleanup school}} – For excessive detail about an educational institution. : {{tlx|Famous}} – For an indiscriminate list of "famous" people associated in some way with a topic. : {{tlx|Localist}} – For local-interest trivia that is unverifiable or otherwise unencyclopedic. ; List cleanup : {{tlx|Prose}} – Suggests converting into prose a section that consists of a list. : {{tlx|Cleanup list}} – For indiscriminate lists that need reduction. : {{tlx|List to table}} – For use where a table would be better than a list. {{Div col end}} <noinclude> {{documentation|content=This is just a trivia-related templates "See also" list that is included in many of these templates' documentation, as well as at [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup#Tagging and improving articles]].}} [[Category:Cleanup templates]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Div col|colwidth=45em}} ; General trivia : {{tlx|Trivia}} – Suggests relocation of the relevant details in a trivia section, to another section (or article). : {{tlx|Trivia section}} – Same as above, but goes at the top of the trivia section, rather than the top of the article. ; Relevance and importance : {{tlx|Off topic}} – For a section that has wandered from the topic of the article : {{tlx|Content}} – For an article or section containing information whose relevance is disputed. : {{tlx|Importance section}} – For a section with information that is simply off-topic for the article, and needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Importance inline}} – For a particular item that is off-topic needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Self-sourcing examples}} – For an article or section with poorly cited examples. : {{tlx|Better source example}} – For a particular instance of a poorly cited example. : {{tlx|Relevance inline}} – For a particular item that doesn't seem to belong in the context at all. : {{tlx|Non sequitur}} – For a namedropping of someone or something the relevance of which may not be clear to the reader. ; Excessive detail : {{tlx|Overly detailed}} – For excessive focus on minute details not of interest to our general readership. : {{tlx|Summarize section}} – For sections that are too detailed and need to be summarized. : {{tlx|Example farm}} – For excessive use of examples. : {{tlx|Too many see alsos}} – For an indiscriminate "See also" section, most of which should be pruned or integrated into the prose. ; Topical trivia : {{tlx|In popular culture}} – For excessive "popular culture" and "media references" sorts of material. : {{tlx|Fiction trivia}} (or {{tlx|In-universe}}) – For too many trivial fictional references (or too much "in-universe" detail). : {{tlx|Long plot}}, {{tlx|All plot}} – For excessively detailed plot summaries. : {{tlx|Cleanup book}}, {{tlx|Cleanup film}} – For excessive detail about particular types of works (other than plot and fictional or in-universe issues). : {{tlx|Game trivia}} – For too much gaming-related trivia. : {{tlx|MOSLOW}} – For list that does not follow the Manual of Style for lists of works, e.g. not in chronological order : {{tlx|Cleanup university}}, {{tlx|Cleanup school}} – For excessive detail about an educational institution. : {{tlx|Famous}} – For an indiscriminate list of "famous" people associated in some way with a topic. : {{tlx|Localist}} – For local-interest trivia that is unverifiable or otherwise unencyclopedic. ; List cleanup : {{tlx|Prose}} – Suggests converting into prose a section that consists of a list. : {{tlx|Cleanup list}} – For indiscriminate lists that need reduction. : {{tlx|List to table}} – For use where a table would be better than a list. {{Div col end}} <noinclude> {{documentation|content=This is just a trivia-related templates "See also" list that is included in many of these templates' documentation, as well as at [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup#Tagging and improving articles]].}} [[Category:Cleanup templates]] </noinclude> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Div col|colwidth=45em}} ; General trivia : {{tlx|Trivia}} – Suggests relocation of the relevant details in a trivia section, to another section (or article). : {{tlx|Trivia section}} – Same as above, but goes at the top of the trivia section, rather than the top of the article. ; Relevance and importance : {{tlx|Off topic}} – For a section that has wandered from the topic of the article : {{tlx|Content}} – For an article or section containing information whose relevance is disputed. : {{tlx|Importance section}} – For a section with information that is simply off-topic for the article, and needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Importance inline}} – For a particular item that is off-topic needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Self-sourcing examples}} – For an article or section with poorly cited examples. : {{tlx|Better source example}} – For a particular instance of a poorly cited example. : {{tlx|Relevance inline}} – For a particular item that doesn't seem to belong in the context at all. : {{tlx|Non sequitur}} – For a namedropping of someone or something the relevance of which may not be clear to the reader. ; Excessive detail : {{tlx|Overly detailed}} – For excessive focus on minute details not of interest to our general readership. : {{tlx|Summarize section}} – For sections that are too detailed and need to be summarized. : {{tlx|Example farm}} – For excessive use of examples. : {{tlx|Too many see alsos}} – For an indiscriminate "See also" section, most of which should be pruned or integrated into the prose. ; Topical trivia : {{tlx|In popular culture}} – For excessive "popular culture" and "media references" sorts of material. : {{tlx|Fiction trivia}} (or {{tlx|In-universe}}) – For too many trivial fictional references (or too much "in-universe" detail). : {{tlx|Long plot}}, {{tlx|All plot}} – For excessively detailed plot summaries. : {{tlx|Cleanup book}}, {{tlx|Cleanup film}} – For excessive detail about particular types of works (other than plot and fictional or in-universe issues). : {{tlx|Game trivia}} – For too much gaming-related trivia. : {{tlx|MOSLOW}} – For list that does not follow the Manual of Style for lists of works, e.g. not in chronological order : {{tlx|Cleanup university}}, {{tlx|Cleanup school}} – For excessive detail about an educational institution. : {{tlx|Famous}} – For an indiscriminate list of "famous" people associated in some way with a topic. : {{tlx|Localist}} – For local-interest trivia that is unverifiable or otherwise unencyclopedic. ; List cleanup : {{tlx|Prose}} – Suggests converting into prose a section that consists of a list. : {{tlx|Cleanup list}} – For indiscriminate lists that need reduction. : {{tlx|List to table}} – For use where a table would be better than a list. {{Div col end}} <noinclude> {{documentation|content=This is just a trivia-related templates "See also" list that is included in many of these templates' documentation, as well as at [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup#Tagging and improving articles]].}} [[Category:Cleanup templates]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Div col|colwidth=45em}} ; General trivia : {{tlx|Trivia}} – Suggests relocation of the relevant details in a trivia section, to another section (or article). : {{tlx|Trivia section}} – Same as above, but goes at the top of the trivia section, rather than the top of the article. ; Relevance and importance : {{tlx|Off topic}} – For a section that has wandered from the topic of the article : {{tlx|Content}} – For an article or section containing information whose relevance is disputed. : {{tlx|Importance section}} – For a section with information that is simply off-topic for the article, and needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Importance inline}} – For a particular item that is off-topic needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Self-sourcing examples}} – For an article or section with poorly cited examples. : {{tlx|Better source example}} – For a particular instance of a poorly cited example. : {{tlx|Relevance inline}} – For a particular item that doesn't seem to belong in the context at all. : {{tlx|Non sequitur}} – For a namedropping of someone or something the relevance of which may not be clear to the reader. ; Excessive detail : {{tlx|Overly detailed}} – For excessive focus on minute details not of interest to our general readership. : {{tlx|Summarize section}} – For sections that are too detailed and need to be summarized. : {{tlx|Example farm}} – For excessive use of examples. : {{tlx|Too many see alsos}} – For an indiscriminate "See also" section, most of which should be pruned or integrated into the prose. ; Topical trivia : {{tlx|In popular culture}} – For excessive "popular culture" and "media references" sorts of material. : {{tlx|Fiction trivia}} (or {{tlx|In-universe}}) – For too many trivial fictional references (or too much "in-universe" detail). : {{tlx|Long plot}}, {{tlx|All plot}} – For excessively detailed plot summaries. : {{tlx|Cleanup book}}, {{tlx|Cleanup film}} – For excessive detail about particular types of works (other than plot and fictional or in-universe issues). : {{tlx|Game trivia}} – For too much gaming-related trivia. : {{tlx|MOSLOW}} – For list that does not follow the Manual of Style for lists of works, e.g. not in chronological order : {{tlx|Cleanup university}}, {{tlx|Cleanup school}} – For excessive detail about an educational institution. : {{tlx|Famous}} – For an indiscriminate list of "famous" people associated in some way with a topic. : {{tlx|Localist}} – For local-interest trivia that is unverifiable or otherwise unencyclopedic. ; List cleanup : {{tlx|Prose}} – Suggests converting into prose a section that consists of a list. : {{tlx|Cleanup list}} – For indiscriminate lists that need reduction. : {{tlx|List to table}} – For use where a table would be better than a list. {{Div col end}} <noinclude> {{documentation|content=This is just a trivia-related templates "See also" list that is included in many of these templates' documentation, as well as at [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup#Tagging and improving articles]].}} [[Category:Cleanup templates]] </noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23631271398|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Div col|colwidth=45em}} ; General trivia : {{tlx|Trivia}} – Suggests relocation of the relevant details in a trivia section, to another section (or article). : {{tlx|Trivia section}} – Same as above, but goes at the top of the trivia section, rather than the top of the article. ; Relevance and importance : {{tlx|Off topic}} – For a section that has wandered from the topic of the article : {{tlx|Content}} – For an article or section containing information whose relevance is disputed. : {{tlx|Importance section}} – For a section with information that is simply off-topic for the article, and needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Importance inline}} – For a particular item that is off-topic needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Self-sourcing examples}} – For an article or section with poorly cited examples. : {{tlx|Better source example}} – For a particular instance of a poorly cited example. : {{tlx|Relevance inline}} – For a particular item that doesn't seem to belong in the context at all. : {{tlx|Non sequitur}} – For a namedropping of someone or something the relevance of which may not be clear to the reader. ; Excessive detail : {{tlx|Overly detailed}} – For excessive focus on minute details not of interest to our general readership. : {{tlx|Summarize section}} – For sections that are too detailed and need to be summarized. : {{tlx|Example farm}} – For excessive use of examples. : {{tlx|Too many see alsos}} – For an indiscriminate "See also" section, most of which should be pruned or integrated into the prose. ; Topical trivia : {{tlx|In popular culture}} – For excessive "popular culture" and "media references" sorts of material. : {{tlx|Fiction trivia}} (or {{tlx|In-universe}}) – For too many trivial fictional references (or too much "in-universe" detail). : {{tlx|Long plot}}, {{tlx|All plot}} – For excessively detailed plot summaries. : {{tlx|Cleanup book}}, {{tlx|Cleanup film}} – For excessive detail about particular types of works (other than plot and fictional or in-universe issues). : {{tlx|Game trivia}} – For too much gaming-related trivia. : {{tlx|MOSLOW}} – For list that does not follow the Manual of Style for lists of works, e.g. not in chronological order : {{tlx|Cleanup university}}, {{tlx|Cleanup school}} – For excessive detail about an educational institution. : {{tlx|Famous}} – For an indiscriminate list of "famous" people associated in some way with a topic. : {{tlx|Localist}} – For local-interest trivia that is unverifiable or otherwise unencyclopedic. ; List cleanup : {{tlx|Prose}} – Suggests converting into prose a section that consists of a list. : {{tlx|Cleanup list}} – For indiscriminate lists that need reduction. : {{tlx|List to table}} – For use where a table would be better than a list. {{Div col end}} <noinclude> {{documentation|content=This is just a trivia-related templates "See also" list that is included in many of these templates' documentation, as well as at [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup#Tagging and improving articles]].}} [[Category:Cleanup templates]] </noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

Relevance and importance : {{tlx|Off topic}} – For a section that has wandered from the topic of the article : {{tlx|Content}} – For an article or section containing information whose relevance is disputed. : {{tlx|Importance section}} – For a section with information that is simply off-topic for the article, and needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Importance inline}} – For a particular item that is off-topic needs removal or relocation to another article. : {{tlx|Self-sourcing examples}} – For an article or section with poorly cited examples. : {{tlx|Better source example}} – For a particular instance of a poorly cited example. : {{tlx|Relevance inline}} – For a particular item that doesn't seem to belong in the context at all. : {{tlx|Non sequitur}} – For a namedropping of someone or something the relevance of which may not be clear to the reader. ; Excessive detail : {{tlx|Overly detailed}} – For excessive focus on minute details not of interest to our general readership. : {{tlx|Summarize section}} – For sections that are too detailed and need to be summarized. : {{tlx|Example farm}} – For excessive use of examples. : {{tlx|Too many see alsos}} – For an indiscriminate "See also" section, most of which should be pruned or integrated into the prose. ; Topical trivia : {{tlx|In popular culture}} – For excessive "popular culture" and "media references" sorts of material. : {{tlx|Fiction trivia}} (or {{tlx|In-universe}}) – For too many trivial fictional references (or too much "in-universe" detail). : {{tlx|Long plot}}, {{tlx|All plot}} – For excessively detailed plot summaries. : {{tlx|Cleanup book}}, {{tlx|Cleanup film}} – For excessive detail about particular types of works (other than plot and fictional or in-universe issues). : {{tlx|Game trivia}} – For too much gaming-related trivia. : {{tlx|MOSLOW}} – For list that does not follow the Manual of Style for lists of works, e.g. not in chronological order : {{tlx|Cleanup university}}, {{tlx|Cleanup school}} – For excessive detail about an educational institution. : {{tlx|Famous}} – For an indiscriminate list of "famous" people associated in some way with a topic. : {{tlx|Localist}} – For local-interest trivia that is unverifiable or otherwise unencyclopedic. ; List cleanup : {{tlx|Prose}} – Suggests converting into prose a section that consists of a list. : {{tlx|Cleanup list}} – For indiscriminate lists that need reduction. : {{tlx|List to table}} – For use where a table would be better than a list. {{Div col end}} <noinclude> {{documentation|content=This is just a trivia-related templates "See also" list that is included in many of these templates' documentation, as well as at [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup#Tagging and improving articles]].}} [[Category:Cleanup templates]]
